pages.raw_text:
Judges at the national apple
show are confronted with hard
work on every hand this year in
deciding on the merits of exhibits
from various districts of the north
west. Apples from the higher al
titudes and non-frigated districts
of both Washington and Oregon
are seemingly unexcelled for uni
formity in size and packing con-
venience, but cannot compare with
the fruit from the lower and irri-
gated districts for dustre and
color.


Fruit growers as rule agree
that the apples from the higher
attitudes, which are now red and
green, while those of the lower al
titudes are rich red and golden yell
low in color at the present time,
will become like the others in
time. This is especially true, it
is claimed, with the Rome Beauty
variety.


Judging has already begun, but
winners will not be made public
until Friday. The contest on good
dPpiCS iCo pi DCt"tSh
Washington and the Rogue River
district of Oregon. Few other
parts of the United States are rep
resented to any great extent, for
despite the fact that the show this
year covers almost twice the area
of the first apple show, held last
year, exhibits were not sent here
from the east as they were then.


The Lafeine apple box, which the
Lafeine box bill proposed to make
the United States. standard, is de-
spised by practically every apple
grower who has seen one of them.


This is a Lafeine apple box
pack it If you can. This is writ
ten on a placard in front of one of
these. troublesome boxes In the
meeting hall of the armory, where
the growers and exhibitors as
sembled this morning. Packing OF
the box is said to be next to inn
possible for fruit growers OF this
part of the country. There are
plenty OF apples piled on the table
